content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Every new vendor spawned another tab, screenshots piled up like bricks, and notes floated about, promises of verification dissolving into digital dust. Meetings ended with that same mantra: “We’ll double check before we approve it.” That wasn’t just slowing things down; key decisions hung frozen while teams frantically searched emails and shared drives for answers. The worst part? Never truly knowing if something vital had slipped through the cracks.
I anticipated another platform intensifying the chaos. Another dashboard, another steep learning curve. We’d attempted replacements before — and invariably ended up with even more manual work than we began with. It was a vicious cycle.
The shift occurred when we stopped viewing risk reviews as isolated documents. Instead, we consolidated everything into one place. I invested in a Plustek Mobile Scanner — needed to digitize the last remaining paper records that circulated endlessly. Suddenly, every decision had documented proof; conversations shortened significantly, and approvals ceased feeling like educated guesses — mere hopeful estimations.
Turns out, I was right.
It wasn’t about speed alone. It was finally understanding why a choice had been correct — solid justification, not just blind faith.
Here’s what nobody tells you: the biggest hurdle wasn’t technology; it was trust.
If this resonated, the full breakdown goes deeper. Follow for more stories like this.
